title: Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple
tagline: Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Magudeswarar Temple in Kodumudi is a large Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in Erode district of Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: The Magudeswarar Temple in Kodumudi is a large Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in Erode district of Tamil Nadu, India. This is the sixth temple in Kongu, the region praised in the Thevaram hymns. it is situated on the banks of the river Kaveri. The sage Agastya is associated with this temple.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kodumudi
topPlaces.description: Kodumudi is a panchayat town and taluk headquarters of Kodumudi taluk in Erode district at state of Tamil Nadu, India. Kodumudi has an average elevation of 144 metres (472 feet).

topPlaces.name: Vengampudur
topPlaces.description: Vengampudur is a panchayat town in Erode taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.

topPlaces.name: Ayyampalayam, Karur
topPlaces.description: Ayyampalayam is a village which comes under Punjai Thottakurichi town Panchayat, in Karur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village mainly depends upon agriculture.

topPlaces.name: Unjalur
topPlaces.description: Unjalur is a panchayat town in Kodumudi taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.

topPlaces.name: Kalingarayan Canal
topPlaces.description: Kalingarayan Canal is a 56.2-mile (90.5 km) long irrigation canal in the Erode district, Tamil Nadu, India. The canal, which irrigates approximately 15,743 acres (6,371 ha) of agricultural land, was constructed by Kongu chieftain Lingaya Kalingaraya Gounder.

topPlaces.name: Chennasamudram
topPlaces.description: Chennasamudram is a panchayat town in Kodumudi taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.

topPlaces.name: Ungampalayam
topPlaces.description: Ungampalayam is a village in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Aathupalayam Dam
topPlaces.description: The Aathupalayam Dam is situated in Karvazhi Village near Thennilai of K. Paramathi Taluk in Karur District. The parched aycut area has not been receiving supplies as the Aathupalayam reservoir had become a storage tank for Orathuppalayam polluted water flowing down the River Noyyal over the past few years.

items.note: Kodumudi is a panchayat town and taluk headquarters of Kodumudi taluk in Erode district at state of Tamil Nadu, India. Kodumudi has an average elevation of 144 metres (472 feet).
Vengampudur is a panchayat town in Erode taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.
Ayyampalayam is a village which comes under Punjai Thottakurichi town Panchayat, in Karur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village mainly depends upon agriculture.
Unjalur is a panchayat town in Kodumudi taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.

Kalingarayan Canal is a 56.2-mile (90.5 km) long irrigation canal in the Erode district, Tamil Nadu, India. The canal, which irrigates approximately 15,743 acres (6,371 ha) of agricultural land, was constructed by Kongu chieftain Lingaya Kalingaraya Gounder.
Chennasamudram is a panchayat town in Kodumudi taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.
Ungampalayam is a village in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

The Aathupalayam Dam is situated in Karvazhi Village near Thennilai of K. Paramathi Taluk in Karur District. The parched aycut area has not been receiving supplies as the Aathupalayam reservoir had become a storage tank for Orathuppalayam polluted water flowing down the River Noyyal over the past few years.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (95 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kodumudi Magudeswarar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kodumudi, Vengampudur, Ayyampalayam, Karur, Unjalur, Kalingarayan Canal.
